content: Analyze Phase 3 arb-matcher build. Delegating to Planning (Architect for design analysis) and Validation (QA + Security) in parallel.

Architecture review of arb-matcher. Rating: 4/5. Clean module decomposition, good type design, solid test coverage. 10 concerns identified: O(n*m) scalability, redundant normalization, unused shared_token_count, MarketPairRow placeholder fields, no idempotent TOML loading, no dedup in pipeline, PairStore hardcoded to concrete type, demo CLI data, hardcoded thresholds, MarketPair type unused. 9 recommendations provided.

QA + Security review of arb-matcher. QA Rating: 3/5 — good foundational coverage (22 tests), missing dedup tests, boundary threshold tests, Unicode tests. Security Rating: 4/5 — no exploitable vulnerabilities, SQL injection prevented, safe TOML parsing. Key concerns: duplicate pair insertion logic bug, placeholder close_time causing premature expiration, quadratic DoS potential. 6 priority items for next phase.

Synthesized findings from Planning (Architect) and Validation (QA + Security). Three teams agree on top priorities: pipeline deduplication, TOML idempotency, close_time placeholder fix. Architecture is sound (4/5), security is clean (4/5), QA coverage needs expansion (3/5).
